# Pokémon Chromatic Yellow — Move Overhaul Plan (Complete Move-List Redo)

This is the **design document** for a ground-up redo of the move
lists in **Pokémon Chromatic Yellow** — the companion piece to
`docs/pokemon_reference.md`, describing the
*planned* state rather than the current one. It was generated by
`tools/gen_move_overhaul_plan.py`, which rebuilds every learnset and TM/HM set
from the repository's (pre-overhaul) data using the rules below, and
validates all design constraints. **This plan has been implemented** — see
`tools/apply_move_overhaul.py` for the codegen that applied it, and
`docs/pokemon_reference.md` for the authoritative current state. The
generator scripts parse the pre-overhaul data files, so they can only be
re-run against a checkout from before the overhaul commit.

## Design goals

1. **A complete band grid.** Every one of the 20 attacking types (Stellar
   and ??? excluded) gets exactly one move in each of eight damage bands:

| Band | Power | Acc | Secondary effect | PP |
|------|-------|-----|------------------|----|
| Weak Physical / Weak Special | 35–50 | 100% | none | 30–35 |
| Moderate Physical / Moderate Special | 50–70 | 100% | allowed | 15–25 |
| Strong Physical / Strong Special | 90 | 100% | only small 10% riders where they define the move (elemental punches, Ice Beam, …) | 15 |
| Strongest Physical / Strongest Special | 110–130 | 85% | allowed (incl. recoil drawbacks) | 5–10 |

   That is **160 damaging moves** — a clean, predictable ladder for every
   type, so every Pokémon can be given sensible STAB at every stage of the
   game.
2. **Every vanilla status move survives untouched** (all 55, from Swords
   Dance to Splash), keeping their fork retypes (Growl→Sound, Flash→Light,
   Sand-Attack→Ground, …).
3. **Nine signature moves kept outside the grid** for their unique
   mechanics or field use: Cut, Quick Attack, Slash, Pay Day, Explosion,
   Counter, Seismic Toss, Night Shade, Swift. Plus Struggle.
4. **Only existing animations.** Every new move names a vanilla move whose
   animation it plays. As implemented: the engine indexes
   `AttackAnimationPointers` by move id (and `ReadMove` copies the move
   struct's first byte into `wPlayerMoveNum`, so that byte must stay equal
   to the move's own id) — therefore each move's pointer-table row points
   at the reused vanilla animation script. The 165 vanilla animation
   scripts, and the sound table they index, are untouched and addressed
   via new `ANIM_*` constants.
5. **No two-turn moves.** Charge and recharge mechanics are gone: Fly,
   Dig, Shadow Force, and Solarbeam hit in one turn, and Hyper Beam no
   longer needs a recharge turn. All five sit at the standard 120 BP /
   85% strongest-band line, alongside the other drawback-free nukes
   (Hydro Pump, Megahorn, Crabhammer, …), so no compensating drawback is
   needed. (Fly and Dig keep their field/HM functions — those are
   independent of the battle effect.) Outrage's 2–3-turn rampage lock
   stays: it attacks every turn, so it is a drawback, not a charge.
6. **Only existing effect handlers.** Every move uses an effect constant
   that already exists in `constants/move_effect_constants.asm`; no new
   battle code is required for any move in this plan. The engine's
   charging-turn code paths simply become unused.

**Roster size: 225 moves** (160 grid + 9 signature + 55 status + Struggle).

## Engine constraints and budget

- Move IDs, battle-animation IDs, and the special battle animations
  (`SHOWPIC_ANIM` … `BAIT_ANIM`) share one byte of ID space, and `$FF` is
  reserved (`CANNOT_MOVE`). Today: 165 moves + 37 special anims.
- This plan needs 225 move IDs + 27 special anims = 252 IDs (≤ 254 ✓),
  after **reclaiming 10 unused special-anim IDs** (`ANIM_A8`, `ANIM_B2`–
  `ANIM_B9`, `ANIM_BB`). `ANIM_B1` turned out to be live — it is reached
  as `SHRINKING_SQUARE_ANIM + 1` (the enemy-side Thrash animation) — so it
  was kept.
- Move names are limited to **12 characters** (`MOVE_NAME_LENGTH - 2`);
  every name below fits. PP is capped at 40 by the `move` macro.
- Vanilla moves that survive **keep their current move IDs**, so the
  animation pointer table, SFX table, and grammar tables stay valid for
  them. Cut moves' ID slots are recycled for new moves; the remainder are
  appended after `STRUGGLE`. The animation pointer table gets one entry
  per new ID (pointing at the reused animation), keeping the special
  anims contiguous after `NUM_ATTACKS` exactly as today.

## Implementation notes (as built)

Applied by `tools/apply_move_overhaul.py` (regenerates every data file
below from `gen_move_overhaul_plan.py`'s output) plus a handful of engine
patches:

1. `constants/move_constants.asm` — new ids (surviving moves keep their
   vanilla ids; cut slots are recycled, the rest appended after Struggle);
   `NUM_ATTACKS` = 225; 10 unused special-anim ids removed; `ANIM_*`
   constants name the 165 vanilla animation-script/sound rows. `ROUND` is
   an rgbasm keyword, so the move Round uses the constant `ROUND_M`.
2. `data/moves/moves.asm`, `names.asm`, `categories.asm` — regenerated.
3. `data/moves/animations.asm` — `AttackAnimationPointers` now has one row
   per move id pointing at the (possibly reused) animation script; the
   scripts' `battle_anim` sound bytes were renamed to `ANIM_*`.
4. `data/moves/sfx.asm` — kept at 165 rows (indexed by `ANIM_*`, not move
   id; `assert_table_length NUM_MOVE_ANIMS`).
5. `data/battle_anims/special_effects.asm` — rows follow the animation a
   move reuses (flash/shake effects); user-vanishing explode effects only
   for Explosion itself. `FORCE_PALM` is pinned to Mega Punch's old slot 5
   because the battle core plays animation id 5 as a generic screen shake.
6. `constants/item_constants.asm` — new `add_tm` list; every renamed
   `TM_*`/`*_TMNUM` reference in scripts/marts/prize tables was remapped
   slot-for-slot.
7. `data/pokemon/base_stats/*.asm`, `data/pokemon/evos_moves.asm` — level-1
   moves, TM/HM compatibility, and learnsets regenerated.
8. `data/trainers/special_moves.asm` — cut moves remapped via the
   disposition table. `data/battle/critical_hit_moves.asm` — high-crit
   list now: Karate Chop, Razor Leaf, Crabhammer, Slash, Stone Edge,
   Night Slash.
9. Engine patches: `GetName`'s vanilla bug (TM-name shortcut applied to
   every list type) fixed so move ids above $C4 display correctly;
   Metronome picks from all 225 moves (it assumed Struggle was last);
   Thrash/Bide/Rage lock-in constants remapped (Outrage/Counter); the
   fixed-damage and charge-text handlers dropped their Dragon Rage /
   Psywave / Skull Bash / Sky Attack branches; Leech Seed's drain and the
   confusion self-hit animations now reference Mega Drain and Tackle.
10. Bank layout: `data/pokemon/cries.asm` and `gfx/fishing.asm` moved to
    floating sections ("Battle Engine 6" and "bank1E" were full).
11. `tools/gen_pokemon_docs.py` updated; `docs/pokemon_reference.md`
    regenerated from the new data.

## Full move list

Origin: **vanilla** = kept (possibly retuned), **repurposed** = vanilla
name in a new role, **new** = new move reusing a vanilla animation.
High-crit moves: Karate Chop, Razor Leaf, Crabhammer, Slash, Stone Edge, Night Slash.

## How the learnsets below were built

Rebuilt mechanically from the current data, then hand-tuned:

1. Status moves keep their exact levels (they all still exist).
2. Every damaging move is remapped through the disposition table
   (e.g. Body Slam→Strength, Hyper Fang→Crunch, Sky Attack→Fly).
3. Duplicates introduced by remapping are dropped (first level wins).
4. **STAB guarantees**, using the species' better attacking stat
   (Attack vs. Sp. Atk) to pick physical or special:
   - a damaging move at level 1 (except Abra, Ditto, Magikarp);
   - its primary type's weak-band move by ~L10 if it has no STAB at all;
   - a moderate-band STAB for evolved/final forms (~L22 primary, ~L30
     secondary type);
   - the strong-band (90 BP) STAB for every final form by ~L38–52.
   Strongest-band moves are deliberately TM/high-level-only.
5. Hand additions for flavor (Pikachu's Wild Charge, Gengar's Shadow
   Ball, legendary birds' Hurricane, …).

Levels shown as `1` are known at level 1 (starting moves). TM/HM
compatibility = vanilla compatibility remapped + all TMs matching the
species' own types + themed grants for the brand-new TMs.
